idea-plugin.name: Apple Trace
idea-plugin.description:
Apple Trace — Logcat for iOS, inside Android Studio

Apple Trace brings a Logcat-style live console for iOS Simulators and physical iOS devices directly into Android Studio (and any IntelliJ IDE) on macOS. If you switch between Android and iOS during the day, you no longer need to leave the IDE to inspect what your iPhone or simulator is doing.

Features

  • Live, color-coded log stream from any booted Simulator or connected iPhone / iPad.
  • Auto-discovers running simulators (xcrun simctl) and physical devices (xcrun devicectl / idevice_id).
  • Filter by bundle id (subsystem), process name, and log level (Default, Info, Debug, Error, Fault).
  • Client-side text search across the streamed buffer.
  • Start / Stop / Clear controls, scroll-lock auto-detect.
  • Output is rendered with proper colors per log level — easy to spot Errors and Faults at a glance.

Requirements

  • macOS only (uses Apple's xcrun tooling).
  • Xcode Command Line Tools installed.
  • For physical devices, optionally install libimobiledevice (brew install libimobiledevice) to enable idevicesyslog.

Open the tool window via View → Tool Windows → Apple Trace.
idea-plugin.change-notes:
1.0.0

  • First public release.
  • Live log streaming from iOS Simulators and physical devices.
  • Filters: bundle id (subsystem), process, log level, free-text search.
  • Color-coded console output.
